highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 delivers the Skia m151 engine, allocation-free interop hot paths, and WASM .NET 11 support.
highlights_body: The release tracks Chrome milestone 151 with multiple upstream bug-fix merges, adds zero-copy Span APIs for SKShaper and SKColor parsing, and extends WebAssembly support to .NET 11 via Emscripten 5.0.6.

bullets.detail: Aligns the bundled renderer with Chrome milestone 151, tracking the upstream Skia engine used by Chrome 151 and bringing its full set of rendering improvements into SkiaSharp.
Three rounds of cherry-picked fixes from the chrome/m151 branch keep the bundled engine current with the latest upstream corrections.
Re-enables SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT, which was accidentally dropped in an earlier native build. Apps that render via the CPU raster backend should see normal performance again.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing gains ReadOnlySpan overloads and is faster
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char> to avoid string allocations, and the hex parsing logic itself is measurably faster for all callers.

bullets.lead: Zero-copy Span reads for SKShaper glyph results
bullets.detail: SKShaper glyph buffers are now read through ReadOnlySpan rather than per-element interop calls, removing a hot allocation on every shaping run.

bullets.lead: Allocation-free tag parsing in SKFourByteTag and HarfBuzzSharp.Tag
bullets.detail: Both tag types previously allocated a char[4] on every Parse call. The implementations now use stack-allocated spans, reducing GC pressure in layout-heavy workloads.
An unnecessary unsafe modifier was removed from GRContextOptions, making it usable in projects that do not allow unsafe code.

bullets.lead: Native resource leak in SKImage.FromEncodedData fixed
bullets.detail: SKImage.FromEncodedData(SKData, SKRectI) was leaking the underlying native image handle when the crop rectangle was applied. The handle is now released correctly.
Adds the Emscripten 5.0.6 static libraries required to build and run SkiaSharp in WASM applications targeting .NET 11.
A duplicate SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM macro definition in the Skia submodule caused redefinition warnings on every Emscripten build. The redundant definition is now removed.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: WebAssembly support across two releases: eliminated the SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM redefinition warning from the Skia submodule and added the Emscripten 5.0.6 libraries for .NET 11. Also contributed a cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness.

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151 line with the Skia m151 engine update and the initial round of upstream chrome/m151 bug fixes, plus the WASM build-warning cleanup.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 added the zero-copy SKShaper Span API, allocation-free tag parsing, SKColor ReadOnlySpan overloads, the GRContextOptions cleanup, and a further set of upstream m151 fixes.

harfbuzz_summary: Adds the Emscripten 5.0.6 libraries that include the updated HarfBuzz 14.2.1 for WASM, and removes hot per-call allocations from HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer.AddUtf8 and HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse.
